reasons: triple rate cut doesn't really affect leasing too much
H1-B drama isn't helpful for hiring
85.4% of debt is fixed so doesn't benefit from rate cuts
below NAV with likely another reason priced in by hedge funds
mentions.text:
@taleino93 @nejatian Hmm, looked into it more. While the Allosaurus pattern is promising, for $HPP, triple rate cut doesn't really affect leasing too much compared to home sales like $Z or $OPEN. It usually encourages hiring but we got the H1-B drama, which isn't helpful either.

They have a lot of debt, 85.4% of the debt is fixed so it doesn't really benefit from rate cuts like companies that use floating interest do on top. 

It is below NAV though, usually there's another reason why it's below NAV that other hedge funds priced in. I don't know enough to say whether one party is wrong or not. 

I'm not too bullish, but maybe the dinosaur sees something I don't.
